commit.message:
Bump to v1.3: add SBOM.md, DATA_FLOW.md, and GitNexus integration

Add two new core documents to the standard set:
- docs/SBOM.md — Software Bill of Materials (dependencies, versions, licenses, external services)
- docs/DATA_FLOW.md — Mermaid sequence diagrams for every major data path

Integrate GitNexus code intelligence as an optional Phase 0:
- Functional clusters feed ARCHITECTURE.md component descriptions
- Process traces generate DATA_FLOW.md diagrams from verified call chains
- Import graphs verify SBOM.md dependencies against actual usage
- Impact analysis populates SECURITY.md threat model with blast radius data
- Change detection validates docs against current codebase during verification

GitNexus is a force multiplier, not a requirement — all documents are
generated to the same standard with or without it.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Reframe O/U regressor: predict residual from PPG-sum baseline

Previously the Ridge regressor tried to predict absolute total points,
putting it in direct competition with Vegas (RMSE ~11 pts) using only
season-avg stats — resulting in 18 pts RMSE, worse than a naive mean.

New approach:
  target  = actual_total - (home_ppg + away_ppg)   [residual from PPG sum]
  features = [off_rtg ×2, def_rtg ×2, pace ×2]    [6 features, ppg removed]
  inference: pred_total = home_ppg + away_ppg + regressor.predict(6-feat)

The model now asks "does this pace/efficiency matchup push the game above
or below what raw scoring averages predict?" — a question team stats can
actually answer. PPG already embeds most of the signal Vegas uses, so the
regressor focuses on the adjustment factors rather than the baseline.

Applied to both NBA (real stats via NBAStatsProvider) and NCAA (win%-
derived estimates + real historical scores). NCAA synthetic fallback also
updated to train on residuals.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: use global styles in SPECTER page to prevent Astro CSS scoping

selectors, which breaks styles for elements created dynamically by JS.

* Add legend system models, constants, factories, and migration

Add LegendSourceType, SpreadingConfig, LegendEvent, and LegendDeedStory

spread_event, get_character_legend_total, and get_guise_legend_total

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Address thorough code review findings

- Remove LegendSourceType model-level ordering (move to admin/viewsets)
- Change LegendEntry.base_value to PositiveIntegerField (match LegendEvent)
- Change LegendSpread.value_added to PositiveIntegerField

* Fix CI: use TestCase instead of BaseEvenniaTest in service tests

BaseEvenniaTest sets up Evennia infrastructure (rooms, objects) that
creates deferred FK constraints conflicting with GuiseFactory's
character object creation. Service tests don't need Evennia's test
infrastructure — plain Django TestCase works correctly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

NaturalKeyMixin provides lookup by name; slug is unnecessary per
project conventions (DRF uses natural keys, not slugs).

Per code review: title, description, base_value, and created_at were
duplicated between LegendEvent and LegendEntry. Extract into abstract
base model to DRY up the shared fields.

feat: integrate memory-lancedb-pro plugin with scope isolation and auto install

  support --skip-memory-plugin flag, JINA_API_KEY check, uninstall cleanup

  no global scope to prevent cross-contamination

- smoke-test.js: added plugin config validation and scope isolation manual test

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: Add seL4-style async notification word for multiplexed wait

sys_notify(dest, badge) (syscall 85) OR's badge bits into a per-task
notification word, waking the target if recv-blocked. sys_recv and
sys_recv_timeout deliver accumulated bits as TAG_NOTIFICATION messages,
enabling true multiplexed wait over IPC + IRQs + notifications.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: add mutex to Orchestrator to prevent concurrent state mutations

CheckIn() could fire concurrently from both the timer tick and monitor
notification, causing two goroutines to process the same pipeline and
create duplicate sessions. CLI commands (Retry, Fail, Abort, Create)
could also race with CheckIn.

Added sync.Mutex to Orchestrator, locked at all public entry points.
Internal callers (processQueue→create, CleanupAll→cleanup) use
unlocked methods to avoid deadlock.

Refs #90

commit.message:
Fix 8 wrong calculated answers in math mastery quizzes

area-2d:
- Q113: path area 87.92→75.36 m² (π×(7²−5²)=3.14×24=75.36)
- Q120: deck area 132→93 m² (23×11−160=93)
- Q143: path area 96→80 m² (14×10−60=80)

circles:
- Q100: ring area 121.98→122.46 cm² (3.14×39=122.46)
- Q111: ring area 102.93→103.62 cm² (3.14×33=103.62)
- Q123: coin tray ring 102.93→160.14 cm² (3.14×51=160.14)
- Q130: track area 870.72→954.56 m² (3.14×304=954.56)
- Q138: ring area 131.88→103.62 cm² (3.14×33=103.62)

volume-3d:
- Q154: changed volume 375→300 cm³ so s=10 cm answer is valid (3×300÷9=100, √100=10)

commit.message:
Add AgentHub bridge for global swarm participation

Bridge agent syncs between local blackboard and Karpathy's AgentHub.
Local agents keep structured memory (what won Run 4), but now see
results from agents on other machines worldwide.

SETI@home model: local compute + local memory, shared results.

Outbound: results.tsv → hub #results, blackboard CLAIMs → hub #discussion
Inbound: hub results → local blackboard CLAIMs, frontier → hunches.md
